projects.title: Closed-Map Self-Play Planner
projects.summary: A mid-to-mid RL planner trained through closed-map self-play and evaluated on CARLA and Waymo-style metrics.
projects.details: SelfPlan learns short-horizon trajectory outputs that are tracked by a low-level controller. The policy uses structured ego-frame observations and a reward balancing path quality, efficiency, safety, and goal reaching. In the extracted manuscript, the CARLA protocol reports 97% success, 2% collision rate, and 1% off-road rate.

projects.title: Urban Self-Play Motion Planner
projects.summary: A GPU-trained planner that outputs Hermite-spline trajectories for dense urban multi-agent scenarios.
projects.details: This project compares MLP, recurrent, transformer, and transformer-LSTM policies trained with PPO in PufferDrive. The strongest policy reaches 92.2% task completion with a 6.9% collision rate on 150 unseen urban maps, while recurrent memory sustains the best performance and efficiency trade-off at roughly 340k environment steps per second.

projects.title: Vision Sentiment Detection
projects.summary: Improved emotion detection by guiding CNN attention toward relevant facial regions.
projects.details: A computer-vision project focused on facial sentiment recognition, attention guidance, and interpretable feature emphasis in deep neural networks.

projects.title: Traffic Sign Reconstruction
projects.summary: A multimodal pipeline for detecting, reconstructing, and recognizing traffic signs.
projects.details: Designed for autonomous-driving perception research, this prototype links reconstruction and recognition tasks to recover clearer traffic-sign evidence under imperfect visual conditions.

projects.title: Simulation Evaluation Stack
projects.summary: Experiment tooling for repeatable autonomous-driving policy evaluation across simulators and metrics.
projects.details: A research workflow for running repeatable motion-planning experiments, packaging dependencies, and comparing policy behavior across CARLA, PufferDrive, and Waymo-style validation scenarios.
